title: festuca cinerea subsp. pallens
intro: Festuca cinerea subsp. pallens, also known as the grey fescue or pale grey fescue, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Festuca cinerea subsp. pallens include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to treat various skin issues and promote overall well-being.
